# DOCUMENT 03 — APP FLOW (Navigation & User Journey Map)

# Every panel, every keypress, every state transition

---

## Entry Point

User runs: ./os\_simulator from the build directory. The binary reads config.yaml from the working directory. If config.yaml is missing or malformed, print a clear error to stderr and exit with code 1\. Do not crash silently.

## First Screen

The terminal clears and the full TUI dashboard renders immediately. The simulation starts PAUSED at Cycle 0\. All PCBs show state: READY with their config values loaded. All memory frames show as empty (grey). The command palette at the bottom shows available keys. The user sees the starting state before anything has run.

## Dashboard Layout (120-column terminal minimum)

![][image1]

## Keypress Map — Complete

SPACE Step simulation forward by exactly one clock cycle. Kernel executes one tick. All panels update. Cycle counter increments. If simulation is complete (all PCBs terminated), SPACE does nothing.  
R Reset simulation to Cycle 0\. All PCBs restored to initial config.yaml values. All memory frames cleared. All telemetry counters reset to zero. Simulation returns to PAUSED state. Does NOT re-read config.yaml — uses values loaded at startup.  
Q Graceful quit. Sets simulation\_running \= false. Flushes telemetry ring buffer to CSV. Closes ofstream. Joins kernel thread. Restores terminal to normal state (FTXUI handles this). Prints: "Simulation saved to simulation\_results.csv" to stdout. Exits with code 0\.  
TAB Cycles focus between interactive controls: Focus 1: Frame count slider (right column) Focus 2: Time quantum input (left column header) Focused control shows a highlight border.  
S Switch scheduling algorithm between RR and SRTF. Takes effect on the next clock cycle. Header updates to show new algorithm name. Ready queue is re-sorted if switching to SRTF.  
A Switch page replacement algorithm: FIFO → LRU → CLOCK → OPT → FIFO. Takes effect on the next page fault. Header updates to show new algorithm name. Does NOT clear current frame contents.  
CTRL+C Same as Q. SIGINT handler triggers graceful shutdown.  
Arrow Keys Adjust the currently focused slider/input value. LEFT/RIGHT on frame slider: decrease/increase frame count by 1\. Minimum frames: 1\. Maximum frames: 16\.

## Core User Journey 1 — Observing a Page Fault and Eviction

1. User launches simulator. Dashboard shows Cycle 0, all frames empty.  
2. User presses SPACE. Cycle 1 runs. First process accesses page 1\. Frame \[1\] in the grid fills with "1". Border flashes RED (page fault, cold load). Fault counter increments. TLB miss recorded.  
3. User presses SPACE several more times. Frames fill up. Some accesses hit (border flashes GREEN). Some miss (border flashes RED, old page evicted).  
4. User can see exactly which page was evicted and which took its place.  
5. User presses A to switch from FIFO to LRU. Next fault evicts a different page. The difference is immediately visible in the grid.

## Core User Journey 2 — Watching Deadlock Avoidance

1. A process requests a resource that would create an unsafe state.  
2. In the Concurrency Monitor, the request appears with a red \[DENIED\] label.  
3. The "Deadlocks Prevented" counter in Live Metrics increments.  
4. The process moves to BLOCKED state in the PCB table.  
5. Another process releases its resource. The blocked process re-requests. This time the state is safe. The request is \[GRANTED\] in green. The process returns to READY state.

## Core User Journey 3 — Priority Inheritance

1. Low-priority Process B holds a KernelMutex.  
2. High-priority Process A requests the same mutex. It blocks.  
3. In the Concurrency Monitor, an upward arrow \[^\] appears next to Process B's PID, indicating its priority has been temporarily boosted.  
4. The PCB table shows Process B's "Current Priority" is now higher than its "Base Priority".  
5. Process B finishes its critical section and releases the mutex.  
6. The \[^\] indicator disappears. Process B's current priority reverts to base.  
7. Process A acquires the mutex and continues.

## Panel States

Memory Grid — Empty Frame : shows \[ \- \] in neutral grey Memory Grid — Occupied Frame: shows \[ N \] where N is the page number Memory Grid — Page Hit : border flashes GREEN for 3 render frames then reverts Memory Grid — Page Fault : border flashes RED for 3 render frames then reverts Memory Grid — Evicted Frame : shows new page number immediately after fault  
PCB Table — Process States: READY : white text RUNNING : green text, bold BLOCKED : yellow text TERMINATED : grey text, struck through  
Lock Monitor — Resource States: \[GREEN O\] : resource available \[RED X\] : resource held, shows owning PID \[^\] : priority inheritance active on this lock holder Wait queue : shows \[ PID4 \] \-\> \[ PID2 \] below a locked resource

## Error States

config.yaml not found : stderr message, exit code 1, no TUI opens config.yaml malformed : stderr message with line number, exit code 1 Terminal too narrow (\<100 cols): clear error message asking user to resize, exit 1 Process requests out-of-bounds virtual address: Simulated segfault: PCB moves to TERMINATED state. Red warning in PCB table: "SIGSEGV — out of bounds access". Host process does NOT crash. Simulation continues with remaining processes.  


[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
